scope: Safe reference-only inspection of local zip archives. No archive source code, generated reports, screenshots, metadata XML, package files, or README prose is copied into permanent repo files.
temporary_folder_policy: Remove after validation because the current ignore rules do not ignore this folder.

useful_patterns: Categorize external Salesforce references by topic.
Use contribution and issue templates for public reference maintenance.
Automate link checking for curated resource pages.
anti_patterns: Old CI stack should not be copied.
Curated link lists become stale without recency checks.
Image-heavy reference repos add little coding value.
what_our_repo_should_learn: Keep external reference maps curated, categorized, and periodically checked.
Treat link lists as navigation aids, not technical authority.
zip_reports.ignore_recommendation: Do not use for implementation guidance.

useful_patterns: Standard force-app/main/default metadata shape.
Object-scoped fields, validation rules, record types, layouts, reports, dashboards, sharing rules, FlexiPages, and triggers are represented as related deployment surfaces.
Package scripts can document lint, unit test, formatting, and precommit expectations.
Reports and dashboards require folder and dependency awareness.
anti_patterns: Trigger source appears without visible matching Apex test strategy.
Developer onboarding documentation is shallow.
Some automation concepts may be dated and require current Salesforce verification.
Generated metadata names are not good public examples.
what_our_repo_should_learn: Improve metadata dependency guidance for reports, dashboards, folders, sharing, fields, layouts, and FlexiPages.
Require test strategy notes whenever trigger metadata is present.
Use source structure as a pattern, not business object models.
zip_reports.ignore_recommendation: Use only for source-format and dependency-mapping lessons.

zip_reports.main_technology: iOS Swift WebView UI test sample
zip_reports.salesforce_relevance: Low; mobile auth and container testing reference only
useful_patterns: Native mobile UI tests can validate login and WebView behavior outside desktop browsers.
Authentication and WebView state should be tested in the real container where relevant.
anti_patterns: Minimal documentation.
No Salesforce DX metadata or platform code.
External tutorial-style sample should not become normative guidance without current verification.
what_our_repo_should_learn: Mobile/WebView guidance should require runtime verification beyond desktop browser checks.
zip_reports.ignore_recommendation: Ignore unless a future mobile WebView testing note needs a conceptual reference.

useful_patterns: Browser automation can produce structured execution evidence.
TestNG suites can represent browser or environment matrix execution.
Separating utility, base, and test layers is a useful UI-testing concept.
anti_patterns: Generated screenshots and HTML reports are committed.
Duplicate build-output folders are present.
No README was found.
Config files and automation samples create credential-like risk surfaces.
Older generic UI automation stack should not be treated as Salesforce platform best practice.
what_our_repo_should_learn: Generated UI automation evidence must stay out of public source.
Public safety checks should include reports, screenshots, logs, and test config files.
zip_reports.ignore_recommendation: Use only as an anti-pattern and hygiene reference.

zip_reports.salesforce_relevance: Low for core Salesforce Platform; relevant only to Commerce Cloud-specific work
useful_patterns: Platform-specific mocks can support tests outside the hosted runtime.
CI should run install, test, and lint.
Large JavaScript packages need license and attribution boundaries.
anti_patterns: Large vendored third-party library trees are inappropriate for this knowledge repo.
Old Node and workflow action versions should not be copied.
Commerce Cloud structure can confuse core Apex, LWC, and metadata guidance.
Library-specific docs and snapshots are mostly noise for this repo.
what_our_repo_should_learn: Keep Commerce Cloud references separate from core Salesforce DX guidance.
Use platform mocks as a testing concept, not copied implementation.
zip_reports.recommended_integration_priority: ignore unless Commerce Cloud scope is added
zip_reports.ignore_recommendation: Ignore for current core Salesforce coding knowledge.

useful_patterns: UI automation can capture report and screenshot evidence when kept outside source control.
Suite XML and Maven dependencies show a common browser automation stack.
anti_patterns: Generated logs, reports, screenshots, and test-output files are committed.
No README was found.
Credential-like and test-data risk surfaces appear in automation artifacts.
UI tests appear tightly coupled to generated output and local state.
what_our_repo_should_learn: Never publish generated browser-test output, logs, screenshots, or data files.
Treat Selenium samples as optional evidence tools, not Salesforce coding patterns.
zip_reports.ignore_recommendation: Use only as an anti-pattern and hygiene reference.

useful_patterns: Minimal SFDX project layout can help new-user orientation.
Scratch definition plus project config demonstrate minimum Salesforce DX project metadata.
Small separate example projects can be easier to compare than large samples.
anti_patterns: Generated local Salesforce state appears in the archive.
README content is skeletal.
No meaningful test, lint, deployment, or metadata quality strategy is visible.
what_our_repo_should_learn: Reinforce ignore rules for local Salesforce state.
Quickstart examples need validation commands and quality expectations, not only folder shape.
zip_reports.ignore_recommendation: Use only for minimal project-shape lessons.

useful_patterns: Data-driven UI testing is a concept worth documenting only when sanitized and separated from source.
anti_patterns: Spreadsheet test data is bundled with source.
No README, setup guide, or quality contract.
No Salesforce DX metadata or platform code.
Credential-like and local automation risk surfaces are possible in browser automation samples.
what_our_repo_should_learn: Do not treat spreadsheet-driven UI samples as Salesforce development guidance.
Keep test data and credentials out of public examples.

zip_reports.salesforce_relevance: Medium to high for documentation taxonomy and governance patterns
useful_patterns: Separate standards pages by topic such as bulkification, triggers, testing, security, sharing, deployment, logging, naming, tooling, and source control.
Include contribution, changelog, install, terms, and open-source checklist documents.
Use documentation-site structure when the target is a public docs site.
Treat architecture, release management, and quality standards as first-class documentation.
anti_patterns: Organization-specific governance and role language should not be copied.
Screenshots and diagrams can age quickly or carry context that is not reusable.
Older standards docs need current Salesforce verification before reuse.
what_our_repo_should_learn: Strengthen taxonomy around governance, source control, testing, logging, deployment, and security.
Keep public docs governance separate from implementation examples.
Rewrite open-source release and contribution checklist concepts for this repo.
zip_reports.ignore_recommendation: Use for documentation structure only; verify technical claims elsewhere.

best_patterns_found: Use a standard Salesforce DX source tree as the center of deployable metadata examples.
Pair metadata examples with dependency checks across object, field, layout, FlexiPage, report, dashboard, sharing, and trigger surfaces.
Require documented commands or package scripts for linting, unit tests, formatting, and validation.
Keep external reference maps curated, categorized, and link-checked.
Treat UI automation reports, screenshots, logs, and data files as generated artifacts, not source.
Keep browser/mobile/WebView testing guidance separate from Apex/LWC unit and metadata deployment guidance.
Use platform mocks as a testing concept only when relevant.
Separate documentation governance from implementation examples.
Verify older CI, Node, Java, Selenium, and Salesforce automation patterns before recommending them.
Do not vendor public example repos, third-party libraries, generated files, or screenshots into a knowledge repo.
worst_anti_patterns_found: Committed generated reports, screenshots, logs, and test-output directories.
Generated local Salesforce state committed in example projects.
Automation config and data files with credential-like risk surfaces.
Selenium/TestNG samples without README, setup contract, or security hygiene.
Metadata samples with trigger code but no visible matching Apex test strategy.
Large vendored third-party library trees in a reference archive.
Old CI stacks and dependency versions that should not be copied.
Product-specific README prose used instead of developer onboarding docs.
Organization-specific governance docs that need rewriting before public reuse.
Shallow quickstart repos that show folder shape but not validation or quality gates.
recommended_next_prompt: Using WORKSPACE/ZIP_REFERENCE_ANALYSIS.md and WORKSPACE/ZIP_REFERENCE_ANALYSIS.json, draft public-safe WORKSPACE-only proposed updates for repo hygiene, metadata dependency guidance, UI automation artifact policy, and external reference handling. Do not modify SALESFORCE_KNOWLEDGE yet, do not commit, and do not push.
